replication_study.hypothesis: Among strong democracies (Polity IV ≥ 9), polarization in preferences over government responsibility (measured as the country-level standard deviation of responses to the WVS 'government responsibility' question; sd_gov) is negatively associated with the size of government (general government consumption as a share of total consumption).

data_plan.source_type: Aggregated survey-derived dataset (WVS-based polarization) merged with macro controls and government consumption.
data_plan.wave_or_subset: WVS waves 5–7; strong democracies only (democ variable ranges 9–10). Optional exclusion of wave 7 per analyze.R parameter.
data_plan.sample_size: 31 observations (country-wave records) when keeping all waves; analyses will report results with and without wave 7.
data_plan.access_details: Provided directly in this project under data/original/17/input/replication_data. At runtime, place data.csv at /app/data/data.csv for the analysis script.

qualification.explanation: This dataset contains the same class of polarization construct (WVS-based dispersion) and the same government size outcome used in the original study, along with the long-specification controls. While the precise polarization indicator differs (GOV vs. PRIVATE question), it tests the same mechanism in a comparable strong democracy sample, thus serving as a close, independent sample for replication.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original: Post-registration notes describe polarization from WVS economic policy questions and outcomes such as government consumption (GOVCONS) with long controls (post_registration.json, 'data' and 'method' sections; original_paper.pdf summary confirms WVS-based polarization and GOVCONS). Provided dataset: Includes sd_gov and mean_gov (WVS question on government responsibility), gov_consumption, and controls: africa, laam, asiae, col_uka, col_espa, col_otha, federal, oecd, log_gdp_per_capita, trade_share, age_15_64, age_65_plus (replication_data/data.csv columns; analyze.R model). Both operate in strong democracies (democ ≥ 9 per data summary).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original focal claim uses the 'Private vs. government ownership' WVS question (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json results cite PRIVATE question coefficients). The provided dataset lacks sd_private/mean_private and instead offers sd_gov/mean_gov. The original aggregates mainly across 2000 (and 1995) waves with outcomes around 2003–2005 (post_registration.json), while this dataset includes waves 5–7 (replication_data/data.csv; wave variable) and appears to use later years (e.g., 2010 present in head). The unit in the original is country-level averages; here it is country-wave, yielding 31 observations instead of ~74 countries (post_registration.json vs. replication_data/data.csv).

data_plan.notes: All observations have democ in [9,10], so the dataset is already restricted to strong democracies. The polarization measure is sd_gov instead of sd_private. The code optionally drops wave 7 (drop_wave_7 flag) to better align with the original timeframe. Variable names and coding appear consistent with a long specification design. Ensure data.csv is copied to /app/data before running analyze.R.

steps: Open and run replication_data/analyze.R in R.
Run the script in real mode with and without dropping wave 7 (the file already calls main(mode='real', drop_wave_7=TRUE/FALSE)).
Extract coefficients, robust standard errors (HC1), p-values, sample size, and R-squared from the output files in /app/data.
Primary inference focuses on the coefficient of sd_gov on gov_consumption.
planned_method.models: OLS regression with heteroscedasticity-robust (HC1) standard errors: gov_consumption ~ sd_gov + mean_gov + africa + laam + asiae + col_uka + col_espa + col_otha + federal + oecd + log_gdp_per_capita + trade_share + age_15_64 + age_65_plus.
planned_method.outcome_variable: gov_consumption (general government consumption as a fraction of total consumption).
planned_method.control_variables: africa, laam, asiae, col_uka, col_espa, col_otha, federal, oecd, log_gdp_per_capita, trade_share, age_15_64, age_65_plus.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient on sd_gov in the long-spec OLS.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test with heteroscedasticity-robust (HC1) standard errors using coeftest from lmtest with vcovHC from sandwich.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion (default behavior of lm for rows with missing values).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single primary outcome and focal predictor; no multiple-testing correction planned.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-sided test at alpha=0.05. Support for the hypothesis is indicated by a negative and statistically significant coefficient on sd_gov.

files.analyze.R: R script that reads /app/data/data.csv, optionally drops wave 7, and estimates the 'long' model (gov_consumption on sd_gov with controls). It computes robust (HC1) SEs using lmtest and sandwich, appends Obs. and R-squared, and writes results to /app/data as four files: test_drop_7.txt, test_keep_7.txt, real_drop_7.txt, real_keep_7.txt. Only the 'real' outputs should be used for inference.
files.data.csv: Country-wave dataset with columns: country identifiers, year, democ (9–10), wave (5–7), WVS polarization variable sd_gov and mean_gov, outcome gov_consumption, and long-specification covariates (regional dummies, colonial origins, federal, oecd, log_gdp_per_capita, trade_share, age_15_64, age_65_plus).

codebase.notes: No path edits required; analyze.R adheres to the /app/data I/O policy. Dependencies: lmtest, sandwich. The script includes randomized 'test' runs; ignore 'test_*.txt' outputs when interpreting results. If needed, comment out the test calls to speed execution.

instructions: Ensure data.csv is available at ./data/data.csv on the host so it mounts to /app/data/data.csv in the container.
After completion, collect /app/data/real_drop_7.txt and /app/data/real_keep_7.txt for reporting.
Extract the coefficient on sd_gov, its robust SE and p-value, Obs., and R-squared from the 'real_*' outputs.
comparison_metrics: Sign and significance of the polarization coefficient: original claim expects a negative effect in strong democracies (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf summary).
Magnitude of effect (compare to original 'long' spec magnitudes acknowledging different polarization question).
Robustness across sample definitions (dropping vs. keeping wave 7).
Model fit (R-squared) and consistency of control variable signs with expectations from the original framework.
